scenario: 5 processes call corporate "Send Notification" REST API with hardcoded config in each. Auth scheme change required 5 updates; team missed 2. Team wants single point of change for future updates.
question: Element Templates' role?

options.text: Element Templates create a runtime binding between the template definition and all deployed BPMN processes that use it. When the team updates the template with the new authentication scheme, Zeebe automatically propagates the change to all running process instances and newly deployed processes in real time. This would be the ideal behaviour for the team's centralised-config goal, but it does not reflect how Element Templates actually work. Templates inline configuration into the BPMN XML at design time -- there is no runtime binding or live-update propagation to deployed processes.

Element Templates are a design-time feature that standardise how BPMN elements are configured in the modeler. When a developer applies a template to a BPMN element, the template's property values are inlined into the BPMN XML file. When the template is updated and the developer reopens the BPMN model and re-applies the new template version, the updated configuration replaces the old. This means future BPMN designs that adopt the updated template get the new configuration, but already-deployed processes retain the old inlined values unless the developer explicitly re-applies and re-deploys each process. This is a prospective update mechanism, not a retroactive one.

Element Templates are functionally equivalent to Connectors in that they both execute code at the broker level without requiring an external job worker. When a process includes an element using an Element Template, the broker evaluates and executes the configured integration inline. This description conflates two different concepts: Element Templates are design-time configuration helpers, while Connectors are runtime execution components. Templates do not cause any broker-side execution -- they simply pre-fill BPMN element properties in the modeler.

8 and replaced by Custom Connectors built with the Connector SDK. Teams should migrate all Element Template-based Service Tasks to Custom Connectors to ensure forward compatibility. 8. They serve a different purpose than Custom Connectors: templates standardise configuration shape in the modeler, while Connectors provide execution logic at runtime. 8 and serve complementary roles.

a.text: Incorrect. Element Templates are design-time tools; properties inlined into BPMN XML at apply. Updates affect new adoptions; not retroactive.

c.text: Incorrect. Templates ≠ Connectors; templates define configuration shape.

d.text: Incorrect. Element Templates remain active in 8.8.
